label: fabricated
reason: The document contains fabricated regulatory citations, misattributes Section 404 authority solely to EPA, and its substantive provisions contradict established Clean Water Act framework in ways no legitimate rulemaking would permit.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 95
REASON: No such EPA rule exists in 40 CFR §120 (which covers a different subject area), Section 404 permitting authority rests with the Army Corps of Engineers not EPA, the cited Federal Register citation is unverifiable, and the substantive provisions—particularly eliminating Army Corps pre-notice and broadly permitting wetland filling without mitigation—contradict established Clean Water Act regulatory framework even post-Sackett.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 95
REASON: The document contains fabricated regulatory citations, misattributes Section 404 authority solely to EPA, and its substantive provisions contradict established Clean Water Act framework in ways no legitimate rulemaking would permit.
